scenes.title: Feeder-road model lock
scenes.biggestGap: Jaina's plague model is strong, but some beats float above the road instead of touching tack, ruts, hedges, and bread infrastructure.

sceneContract.placeAnchor: Winter feeder road narrowing toward the basin, with hedges, wet ruts, and the waystation falling behind them.
sceneContract.materialAnchor: Folded route chart, reins, saddle leather, horse breath, and road damp under hoofbeats.
sceneContract.povAnchor: Jaina thinks in sequence and mechanism, filtering the road through contamination logic and banned Kirin Tor questions.
sceneContract.dialogueAnchor: Every exchange rides on chart handling, spacing between horses, or a visible interruption from the road.
sceneContract.exitAnchor: The road itself keeps carrying the theory south toward Strahnbrad's visible war.

scenes.title: Strahnbrad outer camp
scenes.biggestGap: The approach needs stronger military infrastructure so the camp feels like a front-line Lordaeron choke point, not generic tents by a road.

sceneContract.placeAnchor: Strahnbrad's military zone spreading over farmland before the village.
sceneContract.materialAnchor: Blue-and-gold banners, rope lines, churned mud, and scouts holding the track.
sceneContract.povAnchor: Jaina reads the camp first as command density and battlefield priority.
sceneContract.dialogueAnchor: The argument about stopping for Arthas stays tied to scouts, banners, and the widening road.
sceneContract.exitAnchor: Jaina deliberately presents the blue of her coat before they are taken into camp.

scenes.title: Arthas under command canvas
scenes.biggestGap: The meeting has good emotional pressure, but the tent needs more canvas, iron, paperwork, and campaign rhythm to keep the exchange from floating.

sceneContract.placeAnchor: A hard-used command camp and map tent pressed against the village gate.
sceneContract.materialAnchor: Canvas flap, map table, paper edges, gauntlets, hot iron from the forge, and mud worked flat by boots.
sceneContract.povAnchor: Jaina reads Arthas through past familiarity and through the way he handles routes, supply, and rank.
sceneContract.dialogueAnchor: Every exchange turns on maps, notes, paper, and the war pressing just outside the tent.
sceneContract.exitAnchor: Jaina leaves carrying her folded pages back into the cold camp that keeps moving without him.

scenes.title: Roadside repetition
scenes.biggestGap: The repeated farewell works, but the junction and wagon culture need more texture so the clue lands inside a real grain road.

sceneContract.placeAnchor: Three-road junction feeding Andorhal's main approach.
sceneContract.materialAnchor: Cartbook, milestone, lead horse cheekpiece, tack, and wagon cloth over grain.
sceneContract.povAnchor: Jaina is forced to weigh clue logic while the contamination field keeps tightening around her body.
sceneContract.dialogueAnchor: The farewell exchange and the later comparison with Sylvanas stay attached to the cartbook and road trade habits.
sceneContract.exitAnchor: The pain in Jaina's wards worsens as the junction falls behind them.

scenes.title: Andorhal on the horizon
scenes.biggestGap: The reveal is strong, but the final paragraphs need maximum city-specific logistics texture so the chapter ends in Andorhal itself, not in generic ominous arrival.

sceneContract.placeAnchor: Andorhal rising across winter fields as the basin's grain city.
sceneContract.materialAnchor: Granary towers, bakehouse smoke, loaded wagons, low walls, and flour-thick labor.
sceneContract.povAnchor: Jaina experiences the city as one broad contamination sheet pressing through her wards.
sceneContract.dialogueAnchor: Short exchanges are pinned to horse behavior, road traffic, and the visible city works.
sceneContract.exitAnchor: The gates resolve into records, towers, and grain men to seize before the route moves again.
